name: Secret Resolver / Vault (local vault = source of truth)
description: The RETRIEVAL counterpart of I-Config. Because every platform store this fleet uses is WRITE-ONLY (all Vercel env vars are Sensitive; GitHub Actions secrets are write-only), NO platform can be a read source. So a node keeps a LOCAL ENCRYPTED VAULT (inside the §54.2 trust boundary) as the source of truth for secret values, unlocked by ONE root credential (the vault master key). resolve() reads the vault (offline, fast); seed() puts a value in ONCE from its origin (generated/manual/supabase); push() writes the value OUT to the write-only consuming planes. Refuses undeclared keys; refuses a SECRET/PII outside the trust boundary; NEVER 'pulls' a value the vault lacks; emits a value EXACTLY ONCE to stdout and NEVER logs it. I-Config (presence-only) and the vault (retrieval) compose over the same canonical registry.

verbs.vault-init: create the vault dir (0700) + master key (0600) — the one root of trust
verbs.seed: node … seed <KEY> --env <env> --from generated|manual|supabase (put a value in ONCE)
verbs.get: node … get <KEY> --env <env> (read the value from the vault → stdout ONLY)
verbs.push: node … push <KEY> --env <env> --to vercel,github (write the value OUT to write-only planes)
verbs.plan: node … plan <KEY> --env <env> (metadata: origin + push targets, no value)

invoke.output: the secret value on stdout ONLY (get; no trailing newline). seed/push/plan print only status/metadata to stderr — never a value.

observability.outcome: an append-only audit line on stderr (+ optional $DELIVERYOS_SECRET_AUDIT_LOG): key name + timestamp + plane + verdict (RESOLVED|SEEDED|PUSHED|REFUSED|MISSING|ERROR) — NEVER the value; typed non-zero exit on any failure

invoke.errors: typed, fail-closed: UNKNOWN_KEY(2) · PLANE_REFUSED/UNSUPPORTED(3) · NOT_SEEDED(4) · INSECURE_STORE(5) · OUTSIDE_BOUNDARY(6) · MISSING_ROOT_CREDENTIAL(7) · PROVIDER_ERROR(8). Every non-zero path emits NOTHING on stdout.

provider_roles.note: local encrypted store, AES-256-GCM at rest (zero-dep node:crypto), 0600 files under a 0700 dir. The ONLY reliably-readable store. Optional backends (macOS Keychain, age/sops) documented.
All Vercel env vars are Sensitive → the API NEVER returns the value. Push-only; never a read source.

provider_roles.endpoint: gh secret set <KEY> --body - (value on stdin; gh does the libsodium sealed box)
provider_roles.note: GitHub Actions secrets are write-only by design.
DOCUMENTED-only: the legacy JWT secret is not cleanly returned by the Management API — default is a one-time manual seed from the dashboard. Honest refusal, never a faked value.
value created locally at seed time via node:crypto randomBytes, sized per validation_rule (e.g. CRON_SECRET = secret-min:16).

requires_secret.reason: THE single root of trust ('store one'). A node-local 0600 master key (or $DELIVERYOS_VAULT_KEY_B64 / macOS Keychain) that unlocks the vault. NOT a platform token — platforms are write-only. Rotate this + re-encrypt the vault to rotate the root.

composes_with.how: i-config is presence-only (PRESENT|MISSING|INVALID|DRIFTED) over the SAME config-secret-registry; the vault is the retrieval + push half. i-config finds DRIFT/MISSING on a plane; `push` fixes it from the vault. Both honor never-log / trust-boundary.

auto_renewal.behavior: re-mints GOALS_API_TOKEN from the vault-held signing key when within --threshold of expiry; writes it back to the vault; no manual re-issuance ever; never logs a value.

rotation_safety.behavior: with --probe, a 401 from the engine (root rotated at origin) triggers a root re-sync + re-mint; if re-sync is impossible → fail-closed + ALERT (stderr + optional $DELIVERYOS_ALERT_WEBHOOK). Never keeps a dead/faked token.

service_key_seam.design: a dedicated node-controlled HS256 key (origin=generated, vault-only, rotatable) the renewer prefers over AUTH_JWT_SECRET. Follow-on ENGINE change (design note, not implemented): verify aud=service tokens with this second key in parallel, decoupling machine automation from Supabase's asymmetric-key migration. Vault/registry already shaped for it.

registry_extension.retrieval_source: per-key origin for SEEDING the vault: supabase | generated | manual | vault (distinct from source_provider = the consumption plane).
registry_extension.sensitive: true ⇒ the plane stores a write-only value (Vercel Sensitive / GitHub secret) → push-only, never a read source.
registry_extension.consumers: extra planes push() writes the value out to.

governance.ladder: candidate — retrieval + push are security-sensitive; enable on earned trust, not existence (ku-enable-capabilities-on-trust-not-existence). Promotes after an independent VERIFY on the seed/resolve/push/refuse matrix (mocked providers) + a live push smoke on a non-secret key.
governance.security_review: required (ku-sensitive-changes-require-security-review): this capability holds + retrieves + pushes secret values.
